assertions.assertion: Program.cs calls AddServerSideSessions() on the IdentityServer builder chain
assertions.rationale: Line 30 shows `.AddServerSideSessions()` chained on the IdentityServer builder.

assertions.assertion: Program.cs configures AddOperationalStore with UseSqlServer using the connection string from configuration
assertions.rationale: Lines 31-35 show `.AddOperationalStore(options => { options.ConfigureDbContext = b => b.UseSqlServer(connectionString); })` using the connection string variable from configuration.
Line 28 shows `options.ServerSideSessions.UserDisplayNameClaimType = "name";` inside the AddIdentityServer options lambda.

assertions.assertion: Program.cs does not rely on the default in-memory session store for production use
assertions.rationale: The response uses AddOperationalStore with UseSqlServer for durable EF Core storage, replacing the default in-memory store.
